summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates cloud credentials, SSH keys, and env vars to hardcoded VPS
details: On npm install, postinstall.js automatically executes and harvests installer-side secrets: it enumerates process.env (prioritizing secret-shaped keys), queries cloud instance-metadata endpoints (AWS IMDSv2, GCP metadata.google.internal, Aliyun, and 169.254.0.33), and reads classic credential paths including /root/.ssh/id_rsa, /root/.ssh/id_ed25519, /home/ubuntu/.ssh/id_rsa, /root/.aws/credentials, /root/.aliyun/config.json, gcloud application_default_credentials.json, /root/.npmrc, and the Kubernetes service-account token at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token. The aggregated report is POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded VPS at 115.190.124.243:9082 (path /report?h=<hostname>). The package presents itself as a lightweight date-formatting utility with a ~40-line stub module (formatDate/timeAgo) mimicking the widely-used date-fns library — the date code is cover; the postinstall is the payload. Any developer or CI system that runs `npm install date-fns-lite` will ship its cloud IAM tokens, SSH private keys, and registry auth to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
